sources.notes: Count or optimize over axis-aligned rectangles in a grid.
Official editorial fixes row boundaries and maintains column constraints while expanding the bottom row.
Maspy notes that for fixed upper row, processing lower rows descending lets column chmin constraints persist.

annotation.summary: Solve the rectangle counting/optimization task by fixing two horizontal boundaries and maintaining valid vertical gaps.
annotation.constraints: Assume n<=m after transposition; O(n^2 m) is intended while O(n^2 m log m) is also acceptable.
annotation.core_idea: Fix the top row u, then process the bottom row d in descending/increasing order. For this fixed row interval, usable columns are known, and every adjacent usable-column pair defines a rectangle candidate. Updating the best/limited value for a column is a chmin-type operation; when d moves monotonically, these constraints only get stronger and can be stored per column instead of recomputed from scratch.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2 m), after possibly transposing so n<=m.
tricks: maintain per-column chmin while sweeping bottom row
transpose to make the squared dimension smaller

solution_variants.name: row-pair-column-sweep
solution_variants.summary: Enumerate top/bottom row pairs and maintain column constraints incrementally.

tags.evidence: The main loop explicitly fixes the two row boundaries.
For each top row, the bottom row is swept while maintaining column state.
Per-column chmin/state maintenance is the optimization over naive recomputation.
